How to start a private practice: the complete software toolkit

Last updated: January 15, 2025 • 5 min read

How to start a private practice: the complete software toolkit

You finished your paperwork, hung your shingle, and now the couch is ready—but the calendar is empty. Picking the wrong software in week one can cost you hours of note rewrites, denied claims, or worse, no clients at all. Below is the exact 30-day checklist I used to open my solo therapy practice, comparing the three tools every new clinician actually needs: SimplePractice, TherapyNotes, and Headway.

Quick verdict: SimplePractice gives you everything in one login for $69 and a 15-minute setup. Use Headway first if you need referrals fast, then migrate once your schedule fills.
Try SIMPLEPRACTICE free →

1. The 30-day software timeline for a brand-new practice

Day 1–3: Pick your EHR, start CAQH profile
Day 4–7: Load intake templates, build client portal
Day 8–14: Get credentialed (or join Headway and skip ahead)
Day 15–21: Test telehealth, set up Stripe or merchant account
Day 22–30: Soft-launch with two pro-bono sessions, fix kinks, go live

2. Core stack vs. growth stack: what to buy now vs. later

Core (month 1): HIPAA-scheduler, notes, claims, telehealth
Growth (month 6+): Secondary claims, e-prescribe, advanced reporting
You can always upgrade later—every tool below is month-to-month.

3. SimplePractice walkthrough for beginners

Pricing

  • Starter: $29/mo first clinician
  • Essential: $69/mo (includes telehealth, claims, remittance)
  • 30-day free trial, no card; cancel anytime

Features you’ll use on day one

  • Scheduling with Google Calendar sync
  • Unlimited HIPAA-secure telehealth (720p cap)
  • Autogenerated CMS-1500 & UB-04
  • ERA auto-post
  • Client portal with credit-card on file
  • State-specific intake templates
  • Wiley Note Planner integration

Pros

  • Fastest setup wizard—15 minutes from signup to first appointment
  • iOS/Android apps rated 4.8
  • Built-in Stripe merchant: 2.9% + 30¢
  • Live chat support 7 days

Cons

  • Limited custom report builder
  • No secondary claims in starter plan
  • Telehealth bandwidth capped at 720p

Best for

Solo therapists who want everything in one login and plan to take insurance within 90 days.
Try SIMPLEPRACTICE free →

4. TherapyNotes walkthrough for beginners

Pricing

  • Solo: $59/mo first clinician
  • Additional: $30/mo per clinician
  • 30-day free trial, no card

Features you’ll use on day one

  • 200+ note templates (CBT, DBT, EMDR)
  • To-Do list auto-creates from notes
  • Telehealth via HIPAA Bridge (doxy.me engine, $10/mo add-on)
  • ERA auto-post & denial workflow
  • Client portal with secure messaging
  • e-Prescribe optional add-on ($49/mo)
  • State form library (NY, CA, TX, FL)

Pros

  • Best-in-class note compliance reminders
  • Free data migration from most EHRs
  • Unlimited document storage
  • Live US-based phone support

Cons

  • UI looks dated (circa 2015)
  • No mobile app, only mobile web
  • Telehealth not included in base plan

Best for

Clinicians who prioritize bullet-proof documentation and want hand-holding for insurance audits.
Try THERAPYNOTES free →

5. Headway walkthrough for beginners

Pricing

  • $0/mo platform fee
  • 15–25% of collected reimbursement
  • Open-ended contract, 30-day notice

Features you’ll use on day one

  • Instant paneling with Aetna, Cigna, BCBS, Optum
  • Auto-eligibility & benefits check
  • Patient-matching algorithm
  • Integrated telehealth (doxy.me)
  • Claims submission & follow-up handled
  • Weekly direct deposit

Pros

  • Fastest way to get credentialed (2–4 weeks)
  • Built-in referral stream
  • No software learning curve
  • Handles all ERA denials

Cons

  • Lower reimbursement (15–25% cut)
  • Cannot set own fee schedule
  • Limited to Headway’s payer contracts
  • Data portability restrictions (notes export only PDF)

Best for

New grads who need quick referrals and want to outsource billing while building caseload.
Try HEADWAY free →

6. Head-to-head comparison

FeatureSimplePractice EssentialTherapyNotes SoloHeadway
Base monthly cost$69$59$0
Telehealth includedYesNo (+$10)Yes
Insurance claimsSelf-serveSelf-serveDone for you
Reimbursement cut0%0%15–25%
Setup time15 min30 min2–4 wks credentialing
Mobile appNative iOS/AndroidMobile web onlyNone (web only)
Note templates40+200+Generic PDF
Data exportCSV, PDF, JSONCSV, PDFPDF only

7. Who should choose which

Pick SimplePractice if you want the fastest all-in-one setup and plan to bill insurance or private pay from day one.
Pick TherapyNotes if you lose sleep over audit compliance and want every note template imaginable.
Pick Headway if you need referrals yesterday and don’t mind giving up 15–25% while you build to 15+ clients a week.

Mix-and-match is normal: start on Headway, export PDFs, then migrate to SimplePractice when your private-pay waitlist grows.

8. FAQ

Do I need an EHR before I see my first client?

Legally no, but you must have HIPAA-secure intake, note, and storage systems. SimplePractice offers a 30-day free trial that covers all three.

Can I use Headway and SimplePractice together?

Yes. Many clinicians book Headway clients for the first 6–12 months, then export PDF notes and migrate to SimplePractice once their private-pay schedule is full.

Which software handles CAQH credentialing?

Neither SimplePractice nor TherapyNotes submits CAQH for you; they auto-populate the CAQH forms. Headway submits CAQH on your behalf as part of their onboarding.

Is telehealth included in TherapyNotes?

Telehealth is a $10/mo add-on that uses doxy.me’s HIPAA-secure video. SimplePractice includes it in the $69 plan.

Can I bill insurance myself with SimplePractice?

Yes. SimplePractice generates CMS-1500s, submits electronically, and posts ERAs. Starter plan users can purchase claims credits à la carte.

9. Conclusion & next steps

You now have the exact 30-day software checklist I wish I’d had when I started. Open SimplePractice’s 30-day free trial, load your intake forms, and you can book your first client this week. If referrals feel slow, add Headway to the mix and let their algorithm fill the gaps.
Try SIMPLEPRACTICE free →
